title: CollidersAndNon
tex_block_html:
Let $G=(J,V,E,L)$ be a CDMG and $\pi$ a walk in $G$: \[ \pi =\lp v_0 \sus \cdots \sus v_n \rp. \] A node $v_k$, or more precisely, the position $k \in \lC 0,\dots, n \rC$, on the walk $\pi$ is called:

  1. a non-collider on $\pi$, if there is at most one arrowhead pointing towards $v_k$, i.e.\ if it falls into one of the following cases: \[\begin{array}{rccl} \text{ end-node: } & k \in \lC 0,n \rC, \\ \text{ left chain: } & v_{k-1} \hut v_k \hus v_{k+1}, \\ \text{ right chain: } & v_{k-1} \suh v_k \tuh v_{k+1},\\ \text{ fork: } & v_{k-1} \hut v_k \tuh v_{k+1}; \end{array}\]
  2. a collider on $\pi$, if it is of the form: \[v_{k-1} \suh v_k \hus v_{k+1},\] i.e.\ if there are two arrowheads pointing towards $v_k$ on the walk $\pi$.

tex_statement.html:
Let $G = (J, V, E, L)$ be a CDMG (def def-cdmg); throughout we use the notation of def not-cdmg and the edge classifications of def def-edge-relations. Let \[ \pi = (v_0, a_0, v_1, a_1, \dots, v_{n-1}, a_{n-1}, v_n) \] be a walk in $G$ (def def:walks, item i.) for some integer $n \ge 0$, with $v_0, \dots, v_n \in J \cup V$ and $a_0, \dots, a_{n-1} \in E \cup L$ satisfying the walk constraint at every $k \in \{0, 1, \dots, n - 1\}$: \[ \bigl(a_k = (v_k, v_{k+1}) \;\land\; a_k \in E \cup L\bigr) \;\lor\; \bigl(a_k = (v_{k+1}, v_k) \;\land\; a_k \in E\bigr). \]

Arrowhead count at a position. For a position $k \in \{0, 1, \dots, n\}$, the arrowhead count at $v_k$ on $\pi$, denoted $\mathrm{ah}_\pi(k)$, is the cardinality \[ \mathrm{ah}_\pi(k) := \Bigl|\Bigl\{\, i \in \{k - 1, k\} \,\Bigm|\, 0 \le i \le n - 1 \,\land\, a_i \text{ is an edge into } v_k \text{ in } G \,\Bigr\}\Bigr|, \] where ŌĆ£$a_i$ is an edge into $v_k$ in $G$ŌĆØ is in the sense of def def-edge-relations, item ii.; spelled out, for any $i \in \{0, 1, \dots, n - 1\}$ writing $a_i = (e_1, e_2)$, \[ a_i \text{ is an edge into } v_k \quad\iff\quad \bigl(a_i \in E \;\land\; e_2 = v_k\bigr) \;\lor\; \bigl(a_i \in L \;\land\; (e_1 = v_k \,\lor\, e_2 = v_k)\bigr). \] The index set defining $\mathrm{ah}_\pi(k)$ ranges only over the (at most two) walk-incident edges at position $k$, namely $a_{k-1}$ (when $k \ge 1$) and $a_k$ (when $k \le n - 1$); edges of $G$ that are not incident to $v_k$ along $\pi$ are excluded by construction. At an end-position $k \in \{0, n\}$ at most one such index is admissible, so $\mathrm{ah}_\pi(k) \le 1$ automatically (and $\mathrm{ah}_\pi(0) = 0$ on the trivial walk $n = 0$, where neither index is admissible). At an interior position $1 \le k \le n - 1$ both indices are admissible, so $\mathrm{ah}_\pi(k) \in \{0, 1, 2\}$. (The stored-pair test ŌĆ£$a_i$ is an edge into $v_k$ŌĆØ above is ambiguous in two corner configurations admitted by def def-cdmg: at a walk-incident directed self-loop $a_i \in E$ with $v_i = v_{i+1}$, where its $E$-clause fires unconditionally at both adjacent positions $k \in \{i, i + 1\}$; and at a walk-incident writing-mirror walk-step whose underlying stored pair sits simultaneously in $E$ and in $L$, where its $L$-clause fires unconditionally at both adjacent positions regardless of which channel the walk-step actually traverses. The canonical disambiguation at such positions is the side-aware reading of the addition tag [collider_side_aware_walkstep_predicates], spelled out in the Encoding note and Treatment of directed self-loops and writing-mirror walk-steps paragraphs below.)

Encoding note: side-aware reading via a typed walk-step representation. At a walk-step $a_i$ whose endpoints are distinct ($v_i \ne v_{i+1}$), the walk constraint of def def:walks, item i.\ is typically satisfied by a unique channel assignment: the underlying stored pair lives in exactly one of $E$, $L$, in exactly one orientation, and only one walk-constraint disjunct fires. But def def-cdmg admits writing-mirror pairs: the irreflexivity restriction applies to $L$ only, and no graph-theoretic mutual-exclusion is imposed between the directed channel $E$ and the bidirected channel $L$, so a single underlying vertex pair $\{u, v\}$ may simultaneously support a directed edge $(u, v) \in E$ (or $(v, u) \in E$) and a bidirected edge $s(u, v) \in L$. At a walk-step traversing such a writing-mirror pair, more than one channel assignment to $a_i$ satisfies the walk constraint, and the literal stored-pair ŌĆ£$a_i$ is an edge into $v_k$ŌĆØ test of def def-edge-relations, item ii.\ fires its $L$-clause at both adjacent positions $k \in \{i, i+1\}$ irrespective of which channel the walk-step actually traverses. The same kind of literal-test ambiguity arises at a walk-incident directed self-loop $a_i \in E$ with $v_i = v_{i+1} = v$, where both walk-constraint disjuncts apply syntactically and the stored-pair test's $E$-clause fires unconditionally at both adjacent positions.

The side-aware reading introduced by the addition tag [collider_side_aware_walkstep_predicates] resolves both corners by committing to the walk-step's recorded traversal channel as the sole signal for which channel contributes to the arrowhead test at this step. Each walk-step $a_i$ on $\pi$ carries an explicit channel tag $c_i \in \{\textsf{forward-}E, \textsf{backward-}E, \textsf{bidir-}L\}$, recorded as part of $\pi$ at any walk-step where the underlying vertex/edge sequence alone would leave the channel choice ambiguous ŌĆō writing-mirror walk-steps, and directed self-loops where both orientations $v_i \to v_{i+1}$ and $v_{i+1} \to v_i$ satisfy the walk constraint; at all other walk-steps the channel is forced by the walk's data. The per-step contribution of $a_i$ to $\mathrm{ah}_\pi(k)$ at each adjacent position $k \in \{i, i+1\}$ is determined solely by $c_i$, never consulting whether the underlying stored pair also sits in the opposite channel of $G$:

  • $c_i = \textsf{forward-}E$ (underlying $(v_i, v_{i+1}) \in E$, traversed forward $v_i \to v_{i+1}$): contributes $1$ at $k = i + 1$ and $0$ at $k = i$;
  • $c_i = \textsf{backward-}E$ (underlying $(v_{i+1}, v_i) \in E$, traversed backward $v_{i+1} \to v_i$): contributes $1$ at $k = i$ and $0$ at $k = i + 1$;
  • $c_i = \textsf{bidir-}L$ (underlying $s(v_i, v_{i+1}) \in L$): contributes $1$ at both adjacent positions $k \in \{i, i+1\}$.

A directed step ($c_i \in \{\textsf{forward-}E, \textsf{backward-}E\}$) commits to its $E$-channel reading even when a coexisting $L$-edge $s(v_i, v_{i+1}) \in L$ would, under the literal stored-pair test, additionally contribute an arrowhead at the otherwise-zero adjacent position (the writing-mirror case). Symmetrically, a bidirected step ($c_i = \textsf{bidir-}L$) commits to its $L$-channel bidirected reading even when a coexisting $E$-edge is present in $G$. This refinement strictly overrides the literal stored-pair test of def def-edge-relations, item ii.\ at both corner kinds (writing-mirror walk-steps traversed via the $E$-channel, and directed self-loops), and is taken as the canonical reading of $\mathrm{ah}_\pi(k)$ used by the collider/non-collider classification below. On walks that traverse neither corner configuration, the side-aware and literal stored-pair readings coincide pointwise.

The Lean realisation of this definition implements the side-aware reading faithfully via a typed walk-step representation: each walk-step carries a constructor tag ŌĆō one of forward-$E$, backward-$E$, or bidir-$L$ ŌĆō recording the traversal channel directly, alongside typed source/target end indices at the type level. The per-step contribution to $\mathrm{ah}_\pi(k)$ is read off the constructor tag and the type-level ends alone, with no consultation of the opposite channel of $G$: a forward-$E$ step contributes $1$ at the type-level target end and $0$ at the type-level source end; a backward-$E$ step mirrors, contributing $1$ at the type-level source end and $0$ at the type-level target end; a bidir-$L$ step contributes $1$ at both type-level ends. There is no additional opposite-channel $L$-disjunct on the directed branches and no additional $E$-disjunct on the bidirected branch ŌĆō the constructor tag alone carries the side-aware commitment, exactly realising the per-step contribution formula above. In particular, the strict deviation from the literal stored-pair test is preserved at writing-mirror walk-steps traversed via the $E$-channel as well as at directed self-loops.

Classification. The position $k \in \{0, 1, \dots, n\}$ on the walk $\pi$ ŌĆō or, by abuse, the node $v_k$ at position $k$ ŌĆō is called:

  1. a non-collider on $\pi$ iff $\mathrm{ah}_\pi(k) \le 1$;
  2. a collider on $\pi$ iff $\mathrm{ah}_\pi(k) = 2$.

By construction these two classifications are mutually exclusive and jointly exhaustive over $\{0, 1, \dots, n\}$: every position $k$ on $\pi$ is exactly one of a non-collider or a collider on $\pi$.

Reconciliation with the source-block pattern writings. The source block characterises non-colliders by a list of four pattern-cases (end-node, left chain, right chain, fork) glossed as ŌĆ£at most one arrowhead pointing towards $v_k$ŌĆØ, and colliders by a single pattern-case glossed as ŌĆ£two arrowheads pointing towards $v_k$ on the walk $\pi$ŌĆØ. Below we reconcile these visual writings with the count-based classification, taking the count-based classification (the LN's ŌĆ£i.e.ŌĆØ\ gloss) as canonical. The visual writings $v_{k-1} \tuh v_k$, $v_{k-1} \hut v_k$, $v_{k-1} \huh v_k$, $v_{k-1} \suh v_k$, $v_{k-1} \hus v_k$ are unfolded per def not-cdmg, items 2ŌĆō6.

  • end-node ($k \in \{0, n\}$): $\mathrm{ah}_\pi(k) \le 1$ holds automatically, hence the position is a non-collider on $\pi$. (For the trivial walk $n = 0$, the unique position $k = 0 = n$ has $\mathrm{ah}_\pi(0) = 0$ and is a non-collider.)
  • left chain ($1 \le k \le n - 1$; LN writing $v_{k-1} \hut v_k \hus v_{k+1}$): the walk's $a_{k-1}$ is not an edge into $v_k$, while its $a_k$ is an edge into $v_k$. Spelled out via the walk constraint, $a_{k-1} = (v_k, v_{k-1}) \in E$ (a directed edge with arrowhead at $v_{k-1}$ and tail at $v_k$), and either $a_k = (v_{k+1}, v_k) \in E$ or $a_k = (v_k, v_{k+1}) \in L$. Count: $0 + 1 = 1$; non-collider.
  • right chain ($1 \le k \le n - 1$; LN writing $v_{k-1} \suh v_k \tuh v_{k+1}$): the walk's $a_{k-1}$ is an edge into $v_k$, while its $a_k$ is not an edge into $v_k$. Spelled out, either $a_{k-1} = (v_{k-1}, v_k) \in E$ or $a_{k-1} = (v_{k-1}, v_k) \in L$, and $a_k = (v_k, v_{k+1}) \in E$. Count: $1 + 0 = 1$; non-collider.
  • fork ($1 \le k \le n - 1$; LN writing $v_{k-1} \hut v_k \tuh v_{k+1}$): neither walk-incident edge is an edge into $v_k$. Spelled out, $a_{k-1} = (v_k, v_{k-1}) \in E$ and $a_k = (v_k, v_{k+1}) \in E$. Count: $0 + 0 = 0$; non-collider.
  • collider ($1 \le k \le n - 1$; LN writing $v_{k-1} \suh v_k \hus v_{k+1}$): both walk-incident edges $a_{k-1}, a_k$ are edges into $v_k$. Spelled out, $a_{k-1} \in E \cup L$ with right-component $v_k$ if directed (or either component $v_k$ if bidirected), and analogously for $a_k$ with left-component $v_k$ if directed (or either component $v_k$ if bidirected). Count: $1 + 1 = 2$; collider.

The four non-collider patterns realise exactly the interior counts $\{0, 1\}$, and the collider pattern realises exactly the interior count $2$; together with the end-node case they exhaust the positions of $\pi$. The pattern-cases consult walk-incident edges $a_{k-1}, a_k$ only ŌĆō arrowheads contributed by edges of $G$ that are not on $\pi$ at $v_k$ are excluded from $\mathrm{ah}_\pi(k)$ by construction. (This fixes the scope of the LN's gloss ŌĆ£at most one (resp.\ two) arrowheads pointing towards $v_k$ŌĆØ.) For walks that traverse a directed self-loop $a_i \in E$ with $v_i = v_{i+1} = v$ ŌĆō where the literal pattern writings would otherwise admit any of the four conjunctions above simultaneously ŌĆō or a writing-mirror walk-step whose underlying stored pair sits simultaneously in $E$ and in $L$ ŌĆō where the literal pattern writings admit both the directed and the bidirected channel interpretations ŌĆō the recorded walk-traversal channel of $a_i$ ($\textsf{forward-}E$, $\textsf{backward-}E$, or $\textsf{bidir-}L$) selects exactly one pattern per adjacent position, per the side-aware reading of the addition tag [collider_side_aware_walkstep_predicates]; see the Treatment of directed self-loops and writing-mirror walk-steps paragraph below for the per-position contribution.

Treatment of directed self-loops and writing-mirror walk-steps. The literal stored-pair test ŌĆ£$a_i$ is an edge into $v_k$ŌĆØ of def def-edge-relations, item ii.\ is ambiguous in two corner configurations admitted by def def-cdmg but not excluded by the walk constraint of def def:walks, item i.:

  • Directed self-loop walk-steps. A directed self-loop $(v, v) \in E$ is admitted by def def-cdmg (the irreflexivity restriction applies to $L$, not to $E$). At a walk-incident self-loop edge $a_i = (v_i, v_{i+1}) = (v, v) \in E$ with $v_i = v_{i+1} = v$, both walk-constraint disjuncts of def def:walks, item i.\ apply syntactically, and both the LN's literal pattern writings $v_{k-1} \tuh v_k$ and $v_{k-1} \hut v_k$ (def not-cdmg, items 2ŌĆō3) and the stored-pair test collapse to the single condition $(v, v) \in E$, firing unconditionally at both adjacent positions $k \in \{i, i + 1\}$ for which $v_k = v$. The stored-pair count formula above would commit, at such a self-loop traversal, to contribution $1$ from $a_i$ at both adjacent positions, and a single interior position $k$ traversing the self-loop would simultaneously match the ŌĆ£left chainŌĆØ, ŌĆ£right chainŌĆØ, ŌĆ£forkŌĆØ, and ŌĆ£colliderŌĆØ pattern conjunctions of the Reconciliation paragraph above.
  • Writing-mirror walk-steps. At a non-self-loop walk-step ($v_i \ne v_{i+1}$) whose underlying stored pair $(v_i, v_{i+1})$ (or $(v_{i+1}, v_i)$) is in $E$ and whose unordered version $s(v_i, v_{i+1})$ is in $L$ ŌĆō the writing-mirror configuration permitted by def def-cdmg, which does not impose $E \cap L = \emptyset$ ŌĆō the walk constraint of def def:walks, item i.\ is satisfied by more than one channel assignment to $a_i$, and the stored-pair test's $L$-clause fires unconditionally at both adjacent positions $k \in \{i, i + 1\}$ regardless of which channel the walk-step actually traverses. The literal pattern writings of def not-cdmg similarly admit both the directed and the bidirected channel interpretations at such a walk-step. (The writing-mirror self-loop sibling ŌĆō $(v, v) \in E$ and $s(v, v) \in L$ simultaneously ŌĆō is structurally impossible by def def-cdmg's hL_irrefl, which forbids self-pair elements in $L$.)

The canonical disambiguation in both cases is the side-aware reading introduced by the addition tag [collider_side_aware_walkstep_predicates], as spelled out in the Encoding note paragraph above: each walk-step $a_i$ on $\pi$ carries an explicit traversal channel $c_i \in \{\textsf{forward-}E, \textsf{backward-}E, \textsf{bidir-}L\}$, with the choice recorded explicitly at any walk-step where the underlying vertex/edge sequence alone would leave the channel ambiguous, and the per-step contribution to $\mathrm{ah}_\pi(k)$ is read solely off $c_i$. At a directed self-loop $a_i \in E$, the side-aware reading commits the arrowhead contribution to a single adjacent position ŌĆō $k = i + 1$ when $c_i = \textsf{forward-}E$, $k = i$ when $c_i = \textsf{backward-}E$ ŌĆō and contributes $0$ at the other adjacent position; the $\textsf{bidir-}L$ channel cannot fire at a self-loop because def def-cdmg's irreflexivity restriction on $L$ rules out $s(v, v) \in L$ outright, so the strict side-aware refinement at self-loops is preserved by construction. At a writing-mirror walk-step traversed via $c_i \in \{\textsf{forward-}E, \textsf{backward-}E\}$, the side-aware reading ignores the coexisting $L$-edge entirely, contributing only at the directed channel's arrowhead-receiving end (per the per-step contribution formula in the Encoding note); analogously, a writing-mirror walk-step traversed via $c_i = \textsf{bidir-}L$ commits to the bidirected contribution (arrowheads at both adjacent positions) and ignores the coexisting $E$-edge. This explicitly replaces, at both corner kinds, the literal stored-pair test's reading of $\mathrm{ah}_\pi(k)$; on walks that traverse neither a directed self-loop nor a writing-mirror walk-step the side-aware and stored-pair readings coincide pointwise, so no other part of the definition is affected by the refinement. The recorded walk-traversal channel also selects exactly one of the LN's literal $\tuh / \hut / \huh$ pattern conjunctions per walk-step at each adjacent position, resolving the apparent multi-pattern overlap at both corner kinds.

lean_blocks.explanation: This is a `variable` declaration: Lean auto-binds `Node : Type*` and the instance `[DecidableEq Node]` as implicit arguments to every subsequent declaration in the surrounding `CDMG` namespace, sparing us from rewriting the same parameters on `IsCollider` and `IsNonCollider`. The `Type*` notation makes `Node` universe-polymorphic, so callers can instantiate it at any universe level. The `DecidableEq Node` assumption is load-bearing for the ambient `CDMG Node` carrier: the structure stores `J, V, E, L` as `Finset`-valued fields, and `Finset` needs decidable equality on the element type for membership, union, and filter. Stronger structure on `Node` (e.g.\ `Fintype`, `LinearOrder`) is not required here and is deferred to downstream consumers that actually use it. The same `variable` line wraps every prior chapter-3 file (`CDMG.lean`, `CDMGNotation.lean`, `EdgeRelations.lean`, `Walks.lean`), keeping the section-3.3 signatures continuous with the section-3.1 chapter convention.
lean_blocks.code_annotated:
-- Auto-binds `Node` + `DecidableEq Node` as implicits; decidability is required by every `Finset` field of `CDMG Node`.
variable {Node : Type*} [DecidableEq Node]

lean_blocks.explanation: This second `variable` line sits inside `namespace Walk` and auto-binds the ambient CDMG `G : CDMG Node` as an implicit argument to every subsequent declaration in the namespace. It is the Lean encoding of the LN's once-at-the-top quantifier ``let $G = (J, V, E, L)$ be a CDMG'', threaded through `IsCollider` and `IsNonCollider` without each signature having to repeat `{G : CDMG Node}` explicitly. The binder is implicit (`{G}` rather than `(G)`) because downstream consumers always reach into `G` via dot-notation on a walk ŌĆö `p.IsCollider k` rather than `Walk.IsCollider G p k` ŌĆö so the CDMG is recovered from the walk's type `Walk G u v` by unification at the use site. Pairing this with the first helper, the chapter's standard prelude is in place: `Node` provides the abstract vertex carrier with decidable equality, and `G` fixes the ambient graph against which positions on walks are classified.
lean_blocks.code_annotated:
-- Namespace-wide implicit `G`; recovered by unification from each walk's `Walk G u v` type, no explicit binder on the defs.
variable {G : CDMG Node}

lean_blocks.code:
def IsInto : ŌłĆ {u v : Node}, WalkStep G u v ŌåÆ Node ŌåÆ Prop
  | u, v, .forwardE _,  w => w = v Ōł© (s(u, v) Ōłł G.L Ōł¦ (w = u Ōł© w = v))
  | u, v, .backwardE _, w => w = u Ōł© (s(u, v) Ōłł G.L Ōł¦ (w = u Ōł© w = v))
  | u, v, .bidir _,     w => w = u Ōł© w = v
lean_blocks.explanation: `WalkStep.IsInto s w` is the typed-WalkStep transcription of `def_3_3` item ii's ``edge into a node'' primitive: it asks whether the step `s : WalkStep G u v` places an arrowhead at the node `w` when traversed. A `WalkStep` carries its channel as a constructor tag (`.forwardE`, `.backwardE`, `.bidir`) and its endpoints as Type-level indices `u v`, so the per-step classification can be read off by pattern-matching rather than through an `(a : Node ├Ś Node)`-style membership-disjunction. The naming follows the LN's ``edge into $v_k$'' phrasing: `IsInto vk` reads ``has its arrowhead at `vk`''. Three subtleties are absorbed here: (1) tests use node-equality on type indices, so a directed self-loop with `u = v` correctly fires `IsInto v = True`; (2) the writing-mirror union disjunct `s(u, v) Ōłł G.L Ōł¦ ŌĆ”` on `.forwardE` / `.backwardE` preserves constructor-choice invariance when the stored pair simultaneously lives in `G.L`; (3) `.bidir` needs no symmetric E-disjunct because its L-clause already places arrowheads at both endpoints. The sole consumer is `Walk.IsCollider` below.
lean_blocks.code_annotated:
-- `IsInto s w`: does WalkStep `s` place an arrowhead at node `w`? Per-channel pattern match.
def IsInto : ŌłĆ {u v : Node}, WalkStep G u v ŌåÆ Node ŌåÆ Prop
  -- Forward directed edge `u ŌåÆ v`: arrowhead at target `v`; writing-mirror disjunct re-injects L-channel if pair also in `G.L`.
  | u, v, .forwardE _,  w => w = v Ōł© (s(u, v) Ōłł G.L Ōł¦ (w = u Ōł© w = v))
  -- Backward directed edge `v ŌåÆ u`: arrowhead at source-index `u` (target of underlying directed edge); same writing-mirror disjunct.
  | u, v, .backwardE _, w => w = u Ōł© (s(u, v) Ōłł G.L Ōł¦ (w = u Ōł© w = v))
  -- Bidirected edge `s(u, v) Ōłł G.L`: arrowheads at BOTH endpoints; no extra E-disjunct needed (L-clause dominates).
  | u, v, .bidir _,     w => w = u Ōł© w = v

lean_blocks.code:
def HeadAtTarget : ŌłĆ {u v : Node}, WalkStep G u v ŌåÆ Prop
  | _, _, .forwardE _  => True
  | _, _, .backwardE _ => False
  | _, _, .bidir _     => True
lean_blocks.explanation: `WalkStep.HeadAtTarget s` is a side-aware predicate that asks: does the typed walk-step `s : WalkStep G u v` place an arrowhead at its walk-traversal target end `v`? The body is a pure constructor-tag pattern with no node argument and no `G.L`-membership consultation. `.forwardE _` carries a witness $(u, v) \in G.E$ ŌĆö the directed edge runs $u \to v$, so the target `v` is the arrowhead-receiving end (`True`). `.backwardE _` carries $(v, u) \in G.E$ ŌĆö the edge runs $v \to u$, so the walk-traversal target `v` is the tail end (`False`). `.bidir _` carries $s(u, v) \in G.L$ ŌĆö bidirected semantics places arrowheads at both endpoints (`True`). The constructor tag is the SOLE signal: a directed step commits to its $E$-channel reading even at a writing-mirror pair where $s(u, v) \in G.L$ also holds, and `.bidir` commits to the bidirected reading even when a coexisting $E$-edge sits on the same vertex pair. Paired with `HeadAtSource`, this realises the per-step arrowhead-contribution formula at the two adjacent walk positions of `s`.
lean_blocks.code_annotated:
-- Side-aware: does walk-step `s` place an arrowhead at its walk-traversal target `v`? Pure constructor-tag pattern.
def HeadAtTarget : ŌłĆ {u v : Node}, WalkStep G u v ŌåÆ Prop
  -- Forward directed edge `u ŌåÆ v`: arrowhead at target `v`.
  | _, _, .forwardE _  => True
  -- Backward directed edge `v ŌåÆ u`: tail at walk-traversal target `v`; no opposite-channel L-disjunct.
  | _, _, .backwardE _ => False
  -- Bidirected edge `s(u, v) Ōłł G.L`: arrowheads at both endpoints.
  | _, _, .bidir _     => True

lean_blocks.code:
def HeadAtSource : ŌłĆ {u v : Node}, WalkStep G u v ŌåÆ Prop
  | _, _, .forwardE _  => False
  | _, _, .backwardE _ => True
  | _, _, .bidir _     => True
lean_blocks.explanation: `WalkStep.HeadAtSource s` is the mirror of `HeadAtTarget`: it asks whether the walk-step `s : WalkStep G u v` places an arrowhead at its walk-traversal source end `u`. The body is a pure constructor-tag pattern with no node argument and no `G.L`-consultation. `.forwardE _` encodes $(u, v) \in G.E$ running $u \to v$, so the source `u` is the tail (`False`); `.backwardE _` encodes $(v, u) \in G.E$ running $v \to u$, so the source `u` is the arrowhead-receiving end (`True`); `.bidir _` encodes $s(u, v) \in G.L$, contributing arrowheads at both endpoints (`True`). The constructor tag carries the side-aware commitment alone ŌĆö a directed step ignores any coexisting bidirected edge $s(u, v) \in G.L$ even at writing-mirror pairs. At a directed self-loop encoded as `.forwardE _ : WalkStep G v v`, the source-side predicate evaluates to `False` at the loop vertex, distinguishing tail from head precisely where node-equality on type indices cannot. Together with `HeadAtTarget`, this discharges the per-step contribution at adjacent walk positions consumed by `Walk.IsCollider`.
lean_blocks.code_annotated:
-- Side-aware mirror: does walk-step `s` place an arrowhead at its walk-traversal source `u`? Pure constructor-tag pattern.
def HeadAtSource : ŌłĆ {u v : Node}, WalkStep G u v ŌåÆ Prop
  -- Forward directed edge `u ŌåÆ v`: tail at source `u`; no opposite-channel L-disjunct.
  | _, _, .forwardE _  => False
  -- Backward directed edge `v ŌåÆ u`: arrowhead at source-index `u` (target of underlying directed edge).
  | _, _, .backwardE _ => True
  -- Bidirected edge `s(u, v) Ōłł G.L`: arrowheads at both endpoints.
  | _, _, .bidir _     => True

lean_blocks.explanation: This second `variable` line sits inside `namespace Walk` and auto-binds the ambient CDMG `G : CDMG Node` as an implicit argument to every subsequent declaration in the namespace. It is the Lean encoding of the LN's once-at-the-top quantifier ``let $G = (J, V, E, L)$ be a CDMG'', threaded through `IsCollider` and `IsNonCollider` without each signature having to repeat `{G : CDMG Node}` explicitly. The binder is implicit (`{G}` rather than `(G)`) because downstream consumers always reach into `G` via dot-notation on a walk ŌĆö `p.IsCollider k` rather than `Walk.IsCollider G p k` ŌĆö so the CDMG is recovered from the walk's type `Walk G u v` by unification at the use site. Pairing this with the first helper, the chapter's standard prelude is in place: `Node` provides the abstract vertex carrier with decidable equality, and `G` fixes the ambient graph against which positions on walks are classified.
lean_blocks.code_annotated:
-- Namespace-wide implicit `G`; recovered by unification from each walk's `Walk G u v` type, no explicit binder on the defs.
variable {G : CDMG Node}

lean_blocks.code:
def IsCollider : ŌłĆ {u v : Node}, Walk G u v ŌåÆ ŌäĢ ŌåÆ Prop
  | _, _, .nil _ _, _ => False
  | _, _, .cons _ _ (.nil _ _), _ => False
  | _, _, .cons _ _ (.cons _ _ _), 0 => False
  | _, _, .cons _ sŌéĆ (.cons _ sŌéü _), 1 =>
      sŌéĆ.HeadAtTarget Ōł¦ sŌéü.HeadAtSource
  | _, _, .cons _ _ (p@(.cons _ _ _)), k + 2 =>
      p.IsCollider (k + 1)
lean_blocks.explanation: `Walk.IsCollider p k` is the position-level collider predicate: it holds iff position `k` on the walk `p` has side-aware arrowhead count $\mathrm{ah}_\pi(k) = 2$ ŌĆö both walk-incident steps `s_{k-1}` and `s_k` contribute arrowheads at `v_k`. The five-clause structural recursion on `Walk ├Ś ŌäĢ` encodes the LN's classification: the trivial walk `.nil _ _` and the length-1 walk `.cons _ _ (.nil _ _)` carry no interior position and fall through to `False`; the `k = 0` end-position branch on length-$\ge 2$ walks returns `False`; the `k = 1` branch is the dedicated arrowhead test on the head pair, `sŌéĆ.HeadAtTarget Ōł¦ sŌéü.HeadAtSource`, since `sŌéĆ : WalkStep G _ v_k` has its type-level target at `v_k` and `sŌéü : WalkStep G v_k _` has its type-level source at `v_k`; the `k + 2` branch descends one cons-cell into the tail with index shift `k + 2 ŌåÆ k + 1`. Pattern-match exhaustiveness handles out-of-range `k > p.length` by falling through to `False` automatically. The middle-vertex slot is a wildcard `_` because the WalkStep indices already pin which end of each step sits at `v_k`. Side-awareness, not node-equality, prevents a directed self-loop at the right slot of an `a \to b \to b` walk from being spuriously classified as a collider at `b`.
lean_blocks.code_annotated:
-- Position-level collider: side-aware `ah_ŽĆ(k) = 2` test, structural recursion on `Walk ├Ś ŌäĢ`.
def IsCollider : ŌłĆ {u v : Node}, Walk G u v ŌåÆ ŌäĢ ŌåÆ Prop
  -- Trivial walk `n = 0`: no positions to classify; every `k` is non-collider.
  | _, _, .nil _ _, _ => False
  -- Length-1 walk `n = 1`: only end-positions exist; every `k` is non-collider.
  | _, _, .cons _ _ (.nil _ _), _ => False
  -- End-position `k = 0` on a length-Ōēź2 walk: walk start, automatically non-collider.
  | _, _, .cons _ _ (.cons _ _ _), 0 => False
  -- Interior position `k = 1`: head-pair test using side-aware predicates on `sŌéĆ`, `sŌéü`.
  | _, _, .cons _ sŌéĆ (.cons _ sŌéü _), 1 =>
      -- `sŌéĆ : WalkStep G _ v_k` target at `v_k`; `sŌéü : WalkStep G v_k _` source at `v_k`; no node binder needed.
      sŌéĆ.HeadAtTarget Ōł¦ sŌéü.HeadAtSource
  -- Position `k + 2`: descend one cons-cell with index shift `k + 2 ŌåÆ k + 1`.
  | _, _, .cons _ _ (p@(.cons _ _ _)), k + 2 =>
      p.IsCollider (k + 1)

lean_blocks.code:
def IsNonCollider {u v : Node} (p : Walk G u v) (k : ŌäĢ) : Prop :=
  k Ōēż p.length Ōł¦ ┬¼ p.IsCollider k
lean_blocks.explanation: This block defines the position-level non-collider predicate `Walk.IsNonCollider p k` as the negation of `IsCollider` on the in-range fragment $k \in \{0, \dots, p.length\}$. Since the arrowhead count $\mathrm{ah}_\pi(k)$ lives in $\{0, 1, 2\}$, the LN classification ``$\mathrm{ah}_\pi(k) \le 1$'' is equivalent to ``$\mathrm{ah}_\pi(k) \neq 2$'', i.e.\ to `┬¼ p.IsCollider k`, which makes the LN's mutual-exclusivity statement (every in-range position is exactly one of collider / non-collider) definitional rather than a separate theorem. The explicit bound `k Ōēż p.length` is load-bearing: without it, every out-of-range `k > p.length` would vacuously satisfy `┬¼ p.IsCollider k` (since `IsCollider` is `False` off-range through the Option-`some` conjuncts) and would be misclassified. The asymmetry with the sibling block ŌĆö `IsCollider`'s in-range condition is implicit via Option-membership, while `IsNonCollider` carries it explicitly ŌĆö is the cost of the negation-based encoding; the payoff is that the two predicates are pointwise complementary on $\{0, \dots, p.length\}$ by unfolding alone.
lean_blocks.code_annotated:
-- Non-collider at position `k`: in-range AND not a collider; mutual exclusivity with `IsCollider` becomes definitional.
def IsNonCollider {u v : Node} (p : Walk G u v) (k : ŌäĢ) : Prop :=
  -- Explicit in-range bound; `IsCollider`'s implicit Option-membership scoping is not available on the negation side.
  k Ōēż p.length Ōł¦ ┬¼ p.IsCollider k

addition_to_the_LN:
\textbf{Addition tag \texttt{[collider\_side\_aware\_walkstep\_predicates]}.}
This addition refines the literal LN definition by introducing a \emph{side-aware}
reading of the per-walk-step contribution to $\mathrm{ah}_\pi(k)$ at the two adjacent
walk-positions $k \in \{i, i+1\}$ of a walk-step $a_i$ of $\pi$. The refinement is
authorised because the literal LN definition is ambiguous in two corner configurations:
(i) at a walk-incident directed self-loop $a_i \in E$ with $v_i = v_{i+1}$, where both
walk-constraint disjuncts of def \ref{def:walks}, item~i.\ apply syntactically and the
stored-pair test of def \ref{def-edge-relations}, item~ii.\ fires unconditionally at
both adjacent positions; and (ii) at a walk-step traversing a pair that
simultaneously sits in $E$ as an ordered pair and in $L$ as an unordered pair -- the
\emph{writing-mirror} configuration permitted by def \ref{def-cdmg} (which does not
impose $E \cap L = \emptyset$) -- where the literal stored-pair test's L-disjunct fires
at both adjacent positions regardless of which channel the walk-step actually traverses.
The side-aware reading introduced here resolves both corners by committing to the
walk-step's recorded traversal channel as the \emph{sole} signal for which channel
contributes to the arrowhead test at this step, refining (and strictly overriding) the
literal stored-pair test as the canonical reading of $\mathrm{ah}_\pi(k)$ used by the
collider/non-collider classification.

\medskip\noindent\textbf{(a) Per-step walk-traversal channel and direction are recorded
explicitly.} Each walk-step $a_i$ of $\pi$ carries an explicit walk-traversal channel
$c_i \in \{\textsf{forward-}E, \textsf{backward-}E, \textsf{bidir-}L\}$, with the
convention
\[
    c_i = \textsf{forward-}E \iff \bigl(a_i = (v_i, v_{i+1}) \;\land\; a_i \in E\bigr),
\]
\[
    c_i = \textsf{backward-}E \iff \bigl(a_i = (v_{i+1}, v_i) \;\land\; a_i \in E\bigr),
\]
\[
    c_i = \textsf{bidir-}L \iff a_i \in L \;\text{(taken with}\; a_i = (v_i, v_{i+1})
    \;\text{by convention)}.
\]
At a walk-step whose endpoints are distinct and that does not sit in a writing-mirror
pair, the channel $c_i$ is forced by the data of $\pi$. At a writing-mirror walk-step
($(v_i, v_{i+1}) \in E$ \emph{and} $s(v_i, v_{i+1}) \in L$, with $v_i \ne v_{i+1}$
or $v_i = v_{i+1}$), the choice of $c_i \in \{\textsf{forward-}E, \textsf{backward-}E,
\textsf{bidir-}L\}$ is \emph{recorded explicitly} as part of $\pi$ -- i.e.\ $\pi$
carries strictly more data at such steps than its underlying vertex/edge sequence alone.
At a walk-incident directed self-loop $a_i \in E$ with $v_i = v_{i+1}$, the choice
between $c_i = \textsf{forward-}E$ and $c_i = \textsf{backward-}E$ is similarly
recorded explicitly.

\medskip\noindent\textbf{(b) Side-aware per-step contribution formula at adjacent positions.}
For each walk-step $a_i$ of $\pi$ ($0 \le i \le n - 1$) and each adjacent position
$k \in \{i, i+1\}$, the contribution of $a_i$ to $\mathrm{ah}_\pi(k)$ is determined
\emph{solely} by the recorded channel $c_i$, never consulting whether the underlying
stored pair also sits in the opposite channel:
\begin{itemize}
    \item \emph{Forward-$E$ step} ($c_i = \textsf{forward-}E$): contributes $1$ at the
        walk-traversal target $k = i + 1$ and $0$ at the walk-traversal source $k = i$.
    \item \emph{Backward-$E$ step} ($c_i = \textsf{backward-}E$): contributes $1$ at
        the walk-traversal target $k = i$ and $0$ at the walk-traversal source $k = i + 1$.
    \item \emph{Bidir-$L$ step} ($c_i = \textsf{bidir-}L$): contributes $1$ at both
        adjacent positions $k \in \{i, i+1\}$.
\end{itemize}
A directed step (forward-$E$ or backward-$E$) commits to its $E$-side traversal even
when a coexisting $L$-edge $s(v_i, v_{i+1}) \in L$ would, under the literal stored-pair
test, contribute additional arrowheads at the source position. A bidirected step
($\textsf{bidir-}L$) commits to the $L$-side bidirected contribution even when a
coexisting $E$-edge would, under the literal test, contribute differently. The
arrowhead count $\mathrm{ah}_\pi(k)$ at a position $k \in \{0, 1, \dots, n\}$ is then
the sum of these per-step contributions, summed over the (at most two) walk-incident
steps $i \in \{k - 1, k\} \cap \{0, 1, \dots, n - 1\}$.

\medskip\noindent\textbf{(c) Deviation from the literal stored-pair test is strict at
writing-mirror walk-steps and at directed self-loops.}
The side-aware contribution formula of item~(b) coincides pointwise with the literal
stored-pair ``$a_i$ is an edge into $v_k$'' test of def \ref{def-edge-relations},
item~ii.\ on any walk $\pi$ that traverses neither a writing-mirror configuration nor
a directed self-loop. Where the two readings disagree:
\begin{itemize}
    \item \emph{Directed self-loop steps} ($a_i \in E$ with $v_i = v_{i+1}$, no
        coexisting $L$-edge): the literal test fires unconditionally at both adjacent
        positions, contributing $1 + 1 = 2$; the side-aware reading commits via $c_i$
        to a single position (the walk-traversal target) for the contribution,
        contributing $1$ at that position and $0$ at the other.
    \item \emph{Writing-mirror walk-steps} ($(v_i, v_{i+1}) \in E$ \emph{and}
        $s(v_i, v_{i+1}) \in L$, with $v_i \ne v_{i+1}$): the literal test fires the
        L-disjunct at both adjacent positions regardless of which channel the
        walk-step traverses; the side-aware reading ignores the coexisting $L$-edge
        when $c_i \in \{\textsf{forward-}E, \textsf{backward-}E\}$, contributing only
        at the walk-traversal target of the directed channel.
    \item \emph{Writing-mirror self-loop steps} ($(v, v) \in E$ \emph{and}
        $s(v, v) \in L$): structurally impossible by def \ref{def-cdmg}'s
        \texttt{hL\_irrefl}, which forbids self-pair elements in $L$.
\end{itemize}
The strict-equivalence gate accepts this deviation as the canonical disambiguation of
the LN's ``$\mathrm{ah}_\pi(k)$ counts arrowheads on the walk $\pi$'' phrasing -- a
walk-step \emph{is} the traversal of a single channel of the graph, and an arrowhead
that the walk's channel does not place at $v_k$ is not part of the walk's count, even
when the same pair sits in the opposite channel of the graph as a separate edge object.

\medskip\noindent\textbf{Canonical classification.}
The collider / non-collider classification of def \ref{def:collider_noncollider} is
defined using the side-aware $\mathrm{ah}_\pi(k)$ of items~(a)--(b): position
$k \in \{0, 1, \dots, n\}$ is a non-collider on $\pi$ iff its side-aware arrowhead
count satisfies $\mathrm{ah}_\pi(k) \le 1$, and a collider iff $\mathrm{ah}_\pi(k) = 2$.
The side-aware reading is the canonical disambiguation of the LN's ``i.e.''\ gloss
``at most one arrowhead'' (resp.\ ``two arrowheads pointing towards $v_k$ on the walk
$\pi$''), taking precedence over the literal stored-pair test at both corner kinds
listed in item~(c). On walks avoiding both corners, the classification coincides with
the count obtained from the literal stored-pair test.
design_choices:
**Side-aware per-step arrowhead predicates, not a single node-equality test.** `WalkStep.HeadAtTarget` and `WalkStep.HeadAtSource` are zero-argument predicates that read the per-step contribution off the constructor tag alone. The collider test at position `k = 1` reads `sŌéĆ.HeadAtTarget Ōł¦ sŌéü.HeadAtSource` ŌĆö no middle vertex `v_k` binder ŌĆö because the typed indices `sŌéĆ : WalkStep G _ v_k` and `sŌéü : WalkStep G v_k _` already pin which end of each step sits at `v_k`. A node-equality reading via `IsInto vk` would collapse on directed self-loops: at `.forwardE _ : WalkStep G b b`, the indices `u = v = b` make the test `w = b` fire on both source and target sides indistinguishably, spuriously classifying a position-1 walk `a ŌåÆ b ŌåÆ b` (right step a self-loop) as a collider at `b`. The split prevents that pathology at the type-and-pattern-match level rather than via a runtime guard.

**No opposite-channel disjunct on the side-aware predicates.** The bodies of `HeadAtTarget` and `HeadAtSource` consult neither `G.L` nor `G.E` membership; the constructor tag is the SOLE signal for which channel contributes. A directed step (`.forwardE` or `.backwardE`) commits to its $E$-channel reading even at a writing-mirror pair where $s(u, v) \in G.L$ also holds, and `.bidir` commits to its bidirected reading even when a coexisting $E$-edge is present. This is the strict refinement authorised by the addition tag `[collider_side_aware_walkstep_predicates]`: a walk-step IS the traversal of a single channel, and an arrowhead the walk's channel does not place at `v_k` is not part of the walk's count.

**`IsInto` retained alongside as a coarser, non-side-aware predicate.** `WalkStep.IsInto s w` asks the union-flavoured question "is `w` ANY arrowhead-endpoint of `s`?" via node-equality on type indices plus a writing-mirror $L$-disjunct. It serves consumers that need constructor-choice-invariance across writing-mirror encodings of the same walk-step ŌĆö notably the reversal symmetry lemma `s.reverse.IsInto w Ōåö s.IsInto w` consumed by `claim_3_22`. The two readings coincide on walks avoiding both directed self-loops and writing-mirror walk-steps traversed via the $E$-channel.

**Structural recursion on `Walk ├Ś ŌäĢ`, no `p.edges`-indexing.** `Walk` carries no `.edges` projection, so the five clauses fan out the position semantics: `nil _ _` and `cons _ _ (nil _ _)` send every `k` to `False` (no interior positions); `cons _ _ (cons _ _ _), 0` handles the start end-position; `cons _ sŌéĆ (cons _ sŌéü _), 1` is the head-pair arrowhead test; `cons _ _ (p@(cons _ _ _)), k + 2` recurses into the tail with index shift. Out-of-range `k > p.length` falls through to `False` by pattern-match exhaustiveness; no explicit `1 Ōēż k Ōēż p.length - 1` guard is needed.

**Dedicated position-1 clause, recursion only from `k + 2`.** Position 1 reads both head steps `sŌéĆ` and `sŌéü` simultaneously, both bound at the outer cons-cons pattern. Recursing into the tail at position 1 would drop `sŌéĆ` and lose the left conjunct. The recursion's first opportunity is therefore outer position `k + 2`, where the relevant pair lives in the tail's first two steps.

**`IsCollider` primary, `IsNonCollider` as bounded de Morgan dual.** `IsNonCollider p k := k Ōēż p.length Ōł¦ ┬¼ p.IsCollider k` makes the LN's mutual-exclusivity / joint-exhaustiveness claim definitional on the in-range fragment $\{0, 1, \dots, p.\mathrm{length}\}$ rather than a separate theorem. The explicit bound `k Ōēż p.length` is load-bearing: without it, every out-of-range `k > p.length` would vacuously satisfy `┬¼ p.IsCollider k` and be misclassified. `IsCollider`'s in-range condition is instead implicit in its recursive cons-cell structure ŌĆö the asymmetry is the price for the negation-based encoding.

**`Prop`, not `Bool`.** Walks live at `Type _`; per-position classification at `Prop`. A decidable `Bool` form would demand `DecidableEq Node` discharges at every elaboration site for no downstream benefit ŌĆö `def_3_16` blockability and `def_3_17` $\sigma$-blocking consume these predicates as hypotheses, where `Prop` is the natural fit.

**Namespace-level `variable {G : CDMG Node}` in both `WalkStep` and `Walk`.** Auto-binds `G` as an implicit on every subsequent declaration, encoding the LN's once-at-the-top "let $G = (J, V, E, L)$ be a CDMG" quantifier. Implicit because downstream consumers reach into `G` via dot-notation on a walk (`p.IsCollider k`), recovering `G` by unification from the walk's type `Walk G u v`.
